destinations.evidence: The port now has shared audio and video Phase 2 delivery-result decoders, model-specific video Phase 2 fee calculation, ModelRegistry reads for video pricing fields, and dedicated VIDEO_CALL_PRECOMPILE branches in both payload settlement construction and block verification.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port: builder and verifier no longer price media Phase 2 settlements through generic delivery_result length for audio/video. Audio extracts delivery duration and prices via the audio helper; video extracts width, height, duration_ms plus model/UX pricing metadata and prices via the video helper. Malformed media delivery or input metadata fails settlement construction or block verification instead of falling back to another media path.

related_but_insufficient_code: The original audit observed generic delivery_result length pricing for both media types. Local port code now routes audio and video through shared media-specific fee helpers in both builder and verifier.

notes: Resolved locally by adding the missing video side of the media-specific Phase 2 pricing path; the audio side was already resolved by invariant-0163/0164 work.
rationale: The primary commit restores an explicit audio Phase 2 branch that extracts duration and calls calculate_audio_fees_phase2, removes audio decoding artifacts from the video branch, and adds a dedicated extract_audio_duration helper separate from extract_video_dimensions. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: The diff is explicitly a merge-artifact repair and delimiter fix, so the stable invariant is the media-specific pricing behavior rather than the exact helper names or local structure.
statement: For Phase 2 settlements, audio precompile jobs must extract outputDurationMs from the AudioResponse delivery result, round nonzero duration up to whole seconds, and price via calculate_audio_fees_phase2; video precompile jobs must extract output width, output height, and outputDurationMs from the VideoResponse delivery result, extract model/UX pricing metadata from the original precompile input, and price through the model-specific video path. Malformed extraction for either media type must reject block verification rather than falling back to another media pricing path.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build Phase 2 settlement inputs containing valid AudioResponse and VideoResponse payloads, then assert the verifier computes audio fees from rounded duration seconds and video fees from width/height/duration_ms plus model/UX pricing metadata; also pass truncated or cross-typed delivery results and assert verification fails instead of using a fallback or the wrong media decoder.
summary: Audio/video Phase 2 media-specific pricing is mapped in the port builder and verifier paths.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Feed verifier audio and video Phase 2 delivery settlements with valid media responses and truncated/cross-typed results; the mapped port path should use media-specific pricing and reject malformed inputs.
title: Audio and video Phase 2 pricing decode separately
